channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IUML Candidate from Kondotty Confident as Kerala Assembly Elections Approach
item.description: Ahead of the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, TP Ashraf Ali, the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) candidate from Kondotty constituency, has stated that the party is fully prepared for the polls. Speak...

item.title: Pakistan Offers Mediation to Reduce Middle East Tensions
item.description: Information Minister Attaullah Tarar has confirmed that Pakistan is prepared to play a diplomatic role to help reduce tensions in the Middle East as the conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran worse...

item.title: Macau Woman Briefly Hospitalised After Being Frightened by Humanoid Robot
item.description: A woman in Macau had to be taken to hospital after being startled by a humanoid robot while she was on an evening walk and using her mobile phone. According to police, she did not suffer any physical ...

item.title: Morocco Awarded AFCON 2025 Title After Senegal Forfeit
item.description: Moroccan football fans went wild on the streets after the Confederation of African Football (CAF) Appeals Board officially awarded Morocco the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) title. The drama start...

item.title: Analysis of Nigel Farage's Paid Cameo Messages Reveals Controversial Endorsements
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, has earned over £374,000 through Cameo, a platform where celebrities record personalised video messages. An investigation into more than 4,000 of these videos reveal...

item.title: Sporting CP overturns three-goal deficit to reach Champions League quarter-finals
item.description: Sporting CP make history as dem overcome Bodo/Glimt for UEFA Champions League quarter-finals. For first leg, Bodo/Glimt shock Sporting with 3–0 win, showing say Norwegian side fit perform well even fo...

item.title: Microsoft restructures Copilot teams to accelerate AI and superintelligence development
item.description: Microsoft has recently announced a major reorganisation of its Copilot teams, aiming to unify both commercial and consumer versions of its AI assistant. This step comes as a response to rising competi...

item.title: Two Arrested Over R800,000 Kickbacks Linked to Sedfa in Limpopo
item.description: In Tzaneen, Limpopo, two individuals—a company director and a financial adviser—have been arrested in connection with an alleged corruption and fraud scheme involving over R800,000 in kickbacks from t...

item.title: Sofronio Vasquez releases updated rendition of 'Tadhana' for 'Secrets of Hotel 88' soundtrack
item.description: Filipino singer Sofronio Vasquez has recently recorded a new version of the popular song 'Tadhana' as part of the official soundtrack for the upcoming television series 'Secrets of Hotel 88'. Known fo...

item.title: Public Invited to Give Views on Draft Cryptocurrency Regulations by April 10
item.description: The Kenyan government has opened a public consultation process on the draft Virtual Asset Service Providers Regulations, 2026, giving citizens and stakeholders until April 10 to submit their feedback....

item.title: Islanders defeat Maple Leafs 3-1 with strong first-period power-play performance
item.description: The New York Islanders defeated the Toronto Maple Leafs 3-1 on Tuesday night, thanks in large part to a dominant first-period showing on the power play. Calum Ritchie contributed both a goal and an as...

item.title: Two Arrested in R800k Sedfa Corruption and Kickback Investigation
item.description: Two individuals, a company director and a financial adviser, were arrested in connection with a corruption and fraud case involving over R800,000 in kickbacks from the Small Enterprise Development and...

item.title: Recognising The Contributions and Achievements of Women Globally and in Nigeria
item.description: Dis article dey hail di efforts and achievements of women for all over di world, with special focus on Nigeria and Rivers State. E talk how women dey contribute for family, community, work, business, ...

item.title: Kyle Sandilands to Contest Termination of ARN Radio Contract Under Fair Work Act
item.description: Radio host Kyle Sandilands has had his $100 million contract with ARN terminated following controversy surrounding his morning show with co-host Jackie 'O' Henderson. The termination came after weeks ...

item.title: US Officials Press for Leadership Change in Cuba Amid Nationwide Power Crisis
item.description: On March 17, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio signalled stronger actions towards Cuba, following a nationwide blackout caused in part by recent U.S. sanctions on Ve...

item.title: Thailand’s Gawdland Claims Victory in ‘UK vs the World’ Season 3, Ending UK’s Reign
item.description: Thailand’s Gawdland has made history by winning Season 3 of 'RuPaul's Drag Race: UK vs the World,' putting an end to the United Kingdom’s consecutive winning streak in the competition. This season bro...

item.title: Former Tanzania Spy Chief Reflects on Magufuli’s Legacy of Dedication and Leadership
item.description: At the five-year memorial service held in Chato, former Tanzania Intelligence and Security Service (TISS) director Dr Modestus Kipilimba remembered late President John Pombe Magufuli as a leader whose...

item.title: US Offers Voluntary Departure Program with Travel and Financial Assistance for Illegal Immigrants
item.description: The United States Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has introduced a voluntary self-deportation scheme called Project Homecoming, which provides free flights and an exit bonus of USD 2,600 for ill...

item.title: Comelec clears Marcoleta of election offense; investigates campaign donors for late reporting
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has officially ruled that Senator Rodante Marcoleta did not commit any violation in relation to his campaign contribution disclosures. The en banc adopted the rec...

item.title: Taiwanese Indigenous Dance Performance Restored Before Women's Asian Cup Quarter Final Against China
item.description: A Taiwanese Indigenous dance group was nearly prevented from performing before Taiwan's Women's Asian Cup quarter final against China, but the performance went ahead after intervention from Taiwanese ...
